genre_refs: 'Make America Great Again' slogan riff; iconic 9/11-era ironworker / Lunch atop a Skyscraper imagery
description: A vertical patriotic slogan poster built on a dramatic photograph of a hard-hatted construction ironworker, seen from behind in a red shirt, brown tool belt and dusty work pants, walking out along a steel I-beam high above a hazy city skyline of skyscrapers far below. A large American flag is overlaid semi-transparently across the top of the image, blending into the sky. Centered over the photo in large elegant white serif capitals is the slogan 'AMERICAN WORKERS' / (gold-colored) 'MAKE' flanked by thin gold rule lines / 'AMERICA GREAT' — a clear riff on the 'Make America Great Again' slogan. A circular U.S. Department of Labor seal sits at the bottom center. The composition uses sepia/warm desaturated tones, vertiginous height, the flag, and heroic worker imagery to evoke patriotism, sacrifice and national pride. The propaganda technique fuses MAGA branding with reverence for blue-collar labor. Target audience: working-class Americans, Trump supporters, and a broad patriotic audience.
